name: Adam Smith
title: Professor of Economics
fields: Public Economics · Labor Economics · Taxation & Inequality

bio.short: Adam Smith is a Professor of Economics at Stockholm University working on public and labor economics, with a focus on taxation, inequality, and the welfare state.
bio.standard: Adam Smith is a Professor of Economics at Stockholm University. He combines Swedish administrative register data with quasi-experimental and structural methods to study how tax and social-insurance policy shapes work, wealth, and inequality — from how people respond to marginal tax rates to the long-run effects of parental leave and the consequences of taxing wealth in an open economy. His work has appeared in the American Economic Review, the Quarterly Journal of Economics, the Journal of Political Economy, and Econometrica, and has been covered by The New York Times, the Financial Times, and The Economist.
bio.extended: Adam Smith is a Professor of Economics at Stockholm University, where he has taught since 2021 after positions at the Stockholm School of Economics and Uppsala University. His research sits at the intersection of public and labor economics: he uses Sweden's administrative registers, paired with quasi-experimental and structural methods, to measure how tax and transfer policy affects earnings, savings, and the distribution of income and wealth. His work has appeared in the American Economic Review, the Quarterly Journal of Economics, the Journal of Political Economy, Econometrica, and the Review of Economic Studies, and has been covered by The New York Times, the Financial Times, The Economist, and Bloomberg. His research is supported by a European Research Council Consolidator Grant and the Swedish Research Council. He is a Research Fellow of the CEPR and affiliated with IZA, CESifo, and the Uppsala Center for Fiscal Studies.
